name: Model Evaluation and Benchmarking
tagline: Tests generalization, safety, and reliability beyond training metrics
problem:
Models can look good in training and still fail catastrophically in the real world due to leakage, distribution shift, or adversarial edge cases.

Evaluation solves the honesty problem: define tests that reflect real use, measure uncertainty, and keep results comparable across versions.

The remaining problem for Model Evaluation and Benchmarking is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats model evaluation and benchmarking as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Evaluation uses held-out datasets, targeted stress tests, calibration checks, and red-teaming. It also includes operational monitoring after deployment, because the world changes.

For The Codex, evaluation is a safety layer: it prevents deploying models that are confident, wrong, and unbounded in harm.

In collapse recovery, model evaluation and benchmarking must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Separation of concerns
principles.explanation: Training metrics do not equal real-world performance; use independent tests.

principles.name: Calibration
principles.explanation: Confidence should match correctness; overconfidence is dangerous.

principles.name: Distribution shift
principles.explanation: Performance changes when data changes; monitor and adapt.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Model Evaluation and Benchmarking is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

components.name: Input control point
components.function: Checks materials, tools, information, and environmental conditions before work starts
components.position: At receiving, storage, or setup area
components.madeFrom: Labels, inspection notes, gauges, samples, and quarantine bins
components.criticalNote: Unknown inputs should be isolated until checked.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Test datasets and scenarios
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Use locally relevant scenarios and safety constraints; keep tests offline and versioned.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, confidentiality controls, and consistent scoring.

buildSteps.title: Define evaluation goals
buildSteps.description: Specify what failures matter (safety, accuracy, bias, robustness).
buildSteps.warningNote: Undefined goals lead to cargo-cult benchmarks.

buildSteps.title: Build an evaluation suite
buildSteps.description: Create held-out tests and stress tests; pin versions.

buildSteps.title: Run pre-deploy gates
buildSteps.description: Block releases that regress; require sign-off on safety-critical changes.

buildSteps.title: Monitor post-deploy
buildSteps.description: Track drift and user feedback; retrain responsibly.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Model Evaluation and Benchmarking is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

history.event: Benchmark culture expands with ML competitions and shared datasets.
Safety evaluation and red-teaming become central for large models.

impact:
Evaluation protects users and maintainers from false confidence. It enables responsible iteration and makes models comparable over time.

In The Codex, evaluation is part of the technology, not paperwork: it is the only way to know if an AI system actually works safely.

sources.title: Bishop — Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning
